title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 14 March 2025.

items.title: **Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 3** is officially in development
bullets: Commenting on the announcement, Focus Entertainment deputy CEO Jon Bert reiterated that Space Marine 2 remains the focus for now, with more content to come for that game before Space Marine 3 is released.

items.title: Microsoft is introducing an **Xbox AI** that helps players finish games
bullets: Copilot for Gaming will provide situation-specific tips during gameplay

items.title: **PowerWash Simulator 2** is coming this year to PS5, Xbox Series and PC
bullets: The game isn't currently planned for Nintendo consoles

items.title: **Spectre Divide** developer Mountaintop to shutter just six months post-launch
bullets: CEO says the studio is "out of funding to support the game" and "will close doors at the end of this week"
It's unclear how many people have been impacted by the closure, but LinkedIn states the company employs between 51-200 people.

items.title: **Prytania Media** sues NetEase for defamation, blames it for studio collapse
bullets: The lawsuit—filed on January 3, 2025—seeks $400 million in damages and blames NetEase executives for allegedly leaking confidential financial information to other investors, purportedly in retaliation of the Strains' efforts to help the Chinese conglomerate comply with American regulations.
